title: Global Digital Inclusion Tracker Update — Apr 29, 2026
summary: Amazon Kuiper reached 270 production satellites after its record-weight Atlas V LA-06 launch on April 27, with CEO Andy Jassy confirming mid-2026 commercial service — marking Starlink's first major LEO broadband competitor. The FCC is set to vote on April 30 on satellite spectrum modernization rules that could unlock 700% more satellite broadband capacity, potentially accelerating affordable internet access in underserved regions globally. North Carolina launched its $319 million BEAD broadband initiative on April 23 to connect 93,000 unserved locations by 2030. Two new actors added: FCC Chair Brendan Carr (overseeing the April 30 spectrum vote) and Amazon CEO Andy Jassy (overseeing Project Kuiper). Two new map points added: Cape Canaveral Kuiper launch site and North Carolina BEAD deployment.

title: Global Digital Inclusion Tracker Update — Mon Apr 27, 2026
summary: A United Launch Alliance Atlas V 551 rocket launched 29 Amazon Kuiper broadband satellites from Space Launch Complex-41 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station at 8:53 p.m.

title: Global Digital Inclusion Tracker Update — Sun Apr 26, 2026
summary: FCC Analysis: Spectrum Modernization Order Would Unlock 700% More Satellite Broadband Capacity.

title: Global Digital Inclusion Tracker Update — Thu Apr 23, 2026
summary: North Carolina Launches $319M BEAD Initiative to Connect 93,000 Locations.

title: Global Digital Inclusion Tracker Update — Apr 21, 2026
summary: The ITU Global Connectivity Report 2025 confirmed a historic milestone: 6 billion people (75% of global population) are now online, while 2.2 billion remain unconnected — prompting the ITU to launch a new four-year connectivity plan (2026–2029). SpaceX launched its 1,000th Starlink satellite of 2026 on April 14, bringing the constellation past 7,000 active satellites, while Starlink Direct-to-Cell reached ~10 million monthly users via carrier partnerships. Amazon Kuiper began its Enterprise Beta with 231 production satellites ahead of its FCC-mandated mid-2026 commercial launch. The UK government published its Digital Inclusion Action Plan one-year progress report, and New York City launched its 'Get Online NYC' digital equity campaign. Key KPIs updated to reflect the ITU 2025 data: global penetration revised to 75%, people offline revised to 2.2 billion.
